query:
INSERT INTO works (
  title,
  phaseId,
  series,
  year,
  dimensions,
  technique,
  emotionalRegister,
  imageUrl,
  description,
  journalExcerpt,
  neonReading,
  createdAt,
  updatedAt
) VALUES (
  'Tidal',
  9,
  'Covenant',
  '2025',
  '300cm x 1000cm',
  'Acrylic and spray paint on linen',
  'Gravitas',
  'https://files.manuscdn.com/user_upload_by_module/session_file/310519663243139088/HBPLSsdFbphVwyis.jpg',
  'Seven-panel monumental installation for Swire Properties'' The Headland lobby in Chai Wan, Hong Kong. A site-specific meditation on lunar cycles as respiratory rhythm—seven curved panels depicting moon phases from crescent to full and back, creating a breathing cycle across the curved rotunda wall. The architecture''s oculus ceiling mirrors the central moon sphere. Completed after 6 weeks of on-site painting, December 2025.',
  'The commission asked for lunar phases. I gave them a breathing cycle. Seven panels curving around a rotunda—the architecture itself became the ribcage. Each crescent is a held breath; the central sphere exhales. Sumi ink on Anhui paper at this scale required the pendulum rig to swing for hours. I watched the lines accumulate like tides. The oculus above mirrors the central moon. I didn''t plan that. The building knew.',
  'A site-specific meditation on containment and release. The Swire rotunda demanded work that could hold civic weight without collapsing into decoration. Peter answered with the pendulum''s oldest logic: gravity as collaborator, repetition as revelation. The seven panels operate as a single respiratory system. The flanking crescents—burnt sienna, raw umber, oxidized copper—are the lungs contracting. The central orb is the pause between inhale and exhale, the moment where nothing moves and everything is possible. The moiré interference patterns across each sphere suggest harmonic resonance: sound made visible, time made tactile. This is not lunar symbolism borrowed. It is lunar logic earned—phases that wax and wane across the curved wall the way attention waxes and wanes across a life. The architecture already held a circle in its ceiling; the work completed the circuit, grounding the sky. Monument-scale ink on paper is a declaration: permanence pursued through the most fragile means. That tension is the point. The Covenant series has always asked how structure emerges from chaos. Here, the answer is spatial—the building becomes the vessel-body, and the viewer stands inside it.',
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
command:

Here, the answer is spatial—the building becomes the vessel-body, and the viewer stands inside it.',
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
logs: ERROR 1054 (42S22) at line 1: Unknown column 'series' in 'field list'
